threads should default to physical not logical core count?","user":{"login":"ChrisRackauckas","id":1814174,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE4MTQxNzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1814174?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas","html_url":"https://github.com/ChrisRackauckas","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":61807486,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw2MTgwNzQ4Ng==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/linear%20algebra","name":"linear algebra","color":"f2b3db","default":false,"description":"Linear algebra"},{"id":184008807,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxODQwMDg4MDc=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/multithreading","name":"multithreading","color":"fbca04","default":false,"description":"Base.Threads and related functionality"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":52,"created_at":"2019-09-28T01:08:06Z","updated_at":"2024-10-23T09:29:11Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"On an i7-8550u, OpenBLAS is defaulting to 8 threads. I was comparing to RecursiveFactorizations.jl, and saw the performance is like:

```julia
using BenchmarkTools
import LinearAlgebra, RecursiveFactorization
ccall((:openblas_get_num_threads64_, Base.libblas_name), Cint, ())
LinearAlgebra.BLAS.set_num_threads(4)
BenchmarkTools.DEFAULT_PARAMETERS.seconds = 0.5

luflop(m, n) = n^3÷3 - n÷3 + m*n^2
luflop(n) = luflop(n, n)

bas_mflops = Float64[]
rec_mflops = Float64[]
ns = 50:50:800
for n in ns
    A = rand(n, n)
    bt = @belapsed LinearAlgebra.lu!($(copy(A)))
    rt = @belapsed RecursiveFactorization.lu!($(copy(A)))
    push!(bas_mflops, luflop(n)/bt/1e9)
    push!(rec_mflops, luflop(n)/rt/1e9)
end

using Plots
plt = plot(ns, bas_mflops, legend=:bottomright, lab="OpenBLAS", title="LU Factorization Benchmark", marker=:auto)
plot!(plt, ns, rec_mflops, lab="RecursiveFactorization", marker=:auto)
xaxis!(plt, "size (N x N)")
yaxis!(plt, "GFLOPS")
```

1 thread

![lubenchbnumthreads1](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1814174/65809331-79ef0000-e16a-11e9-8e34-7ee6a5421d29.png)

4 threads

![lubenchbnumthreads4](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1814174/65809337-81160e00-e16a-11e9-9e74-c000b8a469ec.png)

8 threads

![lubench](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1814174/65809340-85422b80-e16a-11e9-99a5-78facf02fb5f.png)

Conclusion: the default that Julia chooses, 8 threads, is the worst, with 1 thread doing better. But using the number of physical cores, 4, is best.

So there were a lot of issues on Discourse and Slack #gripes where essentially "setting BLAS threads to 1 is better than the default!", but it looks like it's because the default should be the number of physical and not logical threads. I am actually very surprised it's not set that way, and so I was wondering why, and also where this default is set (I couldn't find it).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/33409/reactions","total_count":28,"+1":21,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":7,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/33409/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2431470951","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/33409#issuecomment-2431470951","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/33409","id":2431470951,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6Q7U1n","user":{"login":"ImVictorCheng","id":20416460,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNDE2NDYw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20416460?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ImVictorCheng","html_url":"https://github.com/ImVictorCheng","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ImVictorCheng/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ImVictorCheng/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ImVictorCheng/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ImVictorCheng/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ImVictorCheng/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ImVictorCheng/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ImVictorCheng/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ImVictorCheng/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ImVictorCheng/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-23T09:29:10Z","updated_at":"2024-10-23T09:29:10Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Hi, just to follow up, is this issue solved? Does BLAS default to physical core count as of Julia 1.11.1? And is physical core count truely better than logical count?

I just ran some tests on my computer (CPU: i7-7700) and Sys.CPU_THREADS returns 8, BLAS.get_num_threads() returns 4. For my test code the default is indeed faster than forcing BLAS.set_num_threads(8). I'm wondering if this result can be trusted. Thanks!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2431470951/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
